question:
Why do people often find it uncomfortable when someone accepts a compliment with no deflection whatsoever?
A) Direct acceptance of praise violates most cultural norms of modesty, which expect at least token humility even if the compliment is deserved.
B) Compliments are only valid if the recipient disagrees with them.
C) People who accept compliments are statistically more aggressive.
D) A compliment ceases to be true the moment the recipient agrees with it.
